title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.
Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.
Wait for application-specific stable evidence under time and request budgets instead of treating network idle as universal readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 1720 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause.

Inspect the Render Necessity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for render only when static evidence cannot satisfy an authorized stakeholder requirement and retain the decision cause. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity, keep Render Necessity connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rendered Provenance
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-rendered-provenance-f3ape0" that uses case 6994 to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
